optional_accent_budget.ceiling_interpretation: Class-level maxima are ceilings, not instructions to maximize every optional accent class simultaneously.

warnings.detail: assigned optional accents are below target range
optional-accent chapters are below target range

tracking_notes: TROUBLESHOOTING, CITED_EVIDENCE, and EXTERNAL_STORY are tracked outside the optional-accent bucket.
AUTHOR_DISCLOSURE is distinct from AUTHOR_COMMENTARY even when no authored disclosure bank is present.
ANALOGY, METAPHOR, and callback return surfaces are counted downstream from slots/hooks and audits, not from optional accent budgets.

chapters.working_title: The Alarm That Won't Stop Ringing
chapters.thesis: There is a part of you that is always scanning — and you've been so good at living inside that scan that you've mistaken it for just being the kind of person you are.
The alarm didn't malfunction. It learned. At some point, staying alert was the correct strategy, and your nervous system became very, very good at it.

chapters.working_title: The Pattern Running Your Nervous System
chapters.thesis: The alarm does not run randomly. It runs a specific sequence — scan, flag, prepare, check — and once you can see the sequence, you can see where you are inside it at any moment.

chapters.working_title: What The Alarm Actually Does To Your Body
chapters.thesis: The sensations you experience when the alarm runs — the tightness, the racing, the inability to think straight — are not signs that something is going wrong. They are the alarm doing exactly what it was designed to do.

chapters.working_title: What Living On Alert Actually Costs
chapters.thesis: The alarm has a fee. Staying alert full-time is not free, and the cost is not just exhaustion — it is a narrowed life, a shortened present, a set of decisions made by the alarm rather than by you.

Every strategy you've used to manage the alarm — avoiding, preparing, checking, reassurance-seeking — has kept the alarm in charge. The alarm doesn't go quiet because you've satisfied it. It goes quiet because nothing confirmed the threat. The only way to teach the alarm is to let it run.
Practice begins where the alarm is a seven, not where it is a ten. You let it run at that manageable level, and you find out what actually happens next.
There are moments when the alarm does not run at a manageable level. It screams. You can still stay present in those moments. Not comfortably — but present.

chapters.working_title: When Your Body Learns It's Safe
chapters.thesis: The alarm does not update through understanding. It updates through experience — specifically, through the repeated experience of the alarm running and the catastrophe not arriving. You are not teaching your mind new facts. You are teaching your nervous system a new prediction.
The alarm will return. Not because you failed, not because you haven't done the work, but because your body remains responsive. The return of the alarm is information, not relapse.

chapters.working_title: Living With The Alarm On Low
chapters.thesis: You are not going to become a person without an alarm. You are going to become a person who knows when the alarm is running and has a say in what happens next. That is a different person than you were, and it requires updating who you think you are.

This is not the chapter where everything gets better. This is the chapter where you understand what has actually changed, what the alarm is now, and what you can do with a life that is no longer run by it.
